place: Franklin Center CDP, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Franklin Park was originally called Six Mile Run, named after the tributary of the Millstone River that flows through the area.
In the late 1800s, Franklin Park boasted a wagon factory, blacksmith, wheelwright, butcher, hay scales, ice house, school, general store, and about 110 residents.
From 1714 to 1737, Franklin Park hosted Somerset County’s first courthouse and jail before a fire moved the seat to Millstone.
Gifford’s Tavern, where John Quincy Adams and Andrew Jackson once stopped, was replaced in 1874 by the 26‑room Beekman’s Hotel, which burned down in 1929.
